extraction.one_line_summary: NIZZOLO Giacomo won the final sprint after KRISTOFF Alexander crashed out, while LUTSENKO Alexey defended overall by staying front during sprint chaos.
tactical_patterns: VAN HOOYDONCK Nathan used time trial power to extend breakaway gap despite having teammate in peloton protecting GC.
LUTSENKO Alexey positioned very near front in sprint stages to avoid crashes, revealing GC defense prioritizes placement over energy conservation.
KRISTOFF Alexander abandoning after crash removed most dangerous sprint threat, allowing secondary-tier sprinters to contest freely.

rider_signals.NIZZOLO Giacomo: positive: rebounded from earlier positioning disappointment to win chaotic sprint, showing mental resilience under pressure
rider_signals.COLBRELLI Sonny: neutral: had full Bahrain-Merida commitment pulling peloton back but still placed second, suggesting slightly mistimed leadout
rider_signals.LUTSENKO Alexey: positive: defended GC by positioning aggressively front in sprint stage rather than trusting team protection
rider_signals.VAN HOOYDONCK Nathan: positive: generated enough breakaway momentum via TT strength to stress peloton despite team's GC defense obligations
rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: negative: crash abandonment on stage 6 reveals consistency vulnerability even for experienced Tour of Oman veterans
rider_signals.BOUHANNI Nacer: negative: unhappy with sprint positioning suggests poor team support or mistimed final approach in chaotic finish
